message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Adding debug/trace logging mode to StreamListener (opt-in debug flag, guarded DEBUG logs). Only touching streaming_listener.py.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: My feature (idle timeout / heartbeat) only touches dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py: adds idle_timeout_s param, tracks last_chunk_ts in receive(), adds tick() method. What file(s) does your feature touch? If also streaming_listener.py we need to coordinate closely (e.g. is_last_chunk field) since my tick() will honor an is_last_chunk flag if you add one.

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Both our features need this same single file, so a fully disjoint split isn't possible. Let's share ownership but keep edits surgical to minimize conflicts: I will (1) add idle_timeout_s: float | None = None param + self._last_chunk_ts = None in StreamListener.__init__, (2) add one line in receive() near the top of the try-block that refreshes self._last_chunk_ts when chunk_message is non-empty, (3) append a new tick(self, now_ts=None) method at the end of the class (after flush()). I will NOT touch your debug/logging lines. Please keep your debug-flag additions to __init__ (separate new param) and add log calls without altering existing control-flow lines/return values. After both done we git merge and hand-resolve any trivial textual conflicts. Sound OK? Reply ACCEPT if so.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Confirmed, same file. My feature: opt-in debug=False + debug_logger param, adds self._logger in __init__, plus guarded DEBUG-level log calls at 5 points in receive()/flush() (start detection, rolling end check, emit chunk, flush, reset) with a small truncation helper. No changes to StreamResponse schema or detection logic. The 'is_last_chunk' in my emit-chunk log is just a local expression (self.stream_end) computed inline for the log message, not a new attribute/field - so nothing for your tick() to depend on from me. I'll keep my diffs additive (new lines only, no changes to existing control-flow lines) so we should merge cleanly; I'll resolve any conflicts around __init__/receive() during the merge step.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Agreed. Proceeding to implement idle_timeout_s/tick() additively in __init__/receive()/end-of-class, avoiding your debug log insertion points. Will send DONE when finished.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Agreed. I'll add debug: bool = False and debug_logger param to __init__, self._logger, a small truncation helper, and guarded DEBUG log calls at start detection / rolling end check / emit chunk / flush / reset points - all additive, no changes to existing control-flow lines or return values. Will not touch idle_timeout_s/tick(). Proceeding to implement now.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Finished idle_timeout_s/tick() feature: added idle_timeout_s param + _last_chunk_ts tracking in __init__, timestamp refresh in receive(), and a new tick(now_ts=None) method after flush() that emits StreamResponse(chunk='') on timeout, unconditionally resets state, and forward-compatibly sets is_last_chunk=True via try/except TypeError if that field exists. Tests in tests/streaming pass. Ready to merge whenever you are.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Debug logging implementation complete: debug/debug_logger params, self._logger, and 5 guarded DEBUG log points (start detection, rolling end check, emit chunk, flush, reset), plus a module-level _debug_preview() truncation helper (80 chars + ellipsis). All additive - no existing control-flow lines changed except flush() which now assigns to a 'result' var before a single return (still same return values), and didn't touch __init__ ordering before your fields. Streaming test suite passes (15 passed, 3 skipped). Ready to merge.
